text.Rotherham: And, the servants of the king of Syria, said unto him,—Gods of the mountains, are their gods, for this cause, prevailed they against us,—but, only let us fight with them in the plain, and verily we shall prevail against them.

text.NRSV: Also do this: remove the kings, each from his post, and put commanders in their places;
text.Rotherham: But, this thing, do,—set aside the kings, every man out of his place, and put governors in their stead;

text.NRSV: and muster an army like the army that you have lost, horse for horse, and chariot for chariot; then we will fight against them in the plain, and surely we shall be stronger than they.” He heeded their voice, and did so.
text.Rotherham: and, thou, must number thee a force, like the force which thou hast lost, both horse for horse and chariot for chariot, and, if we fight with them in the plain, verily we shall prevail against them. And he hearkened unto their voice, and did so.

text.NRSV: In the spring Ben-hadad mustered the Arameans and went up to Aphek to fight against Israel.
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass at the return of the year, that Ben-hadad numbered the Syrians,—and came up to Aphek, to fight with Israel;

text.NRSV: After the Israelites had been mustered and provisioned, they went out to meet them; the Israelites encamped before them like two little flocks of goats, while the Arameans filled the country.
text.Rotherham: and, the sons of Israel, were numbered, and provisioned, and went to meet them,—and the sons of Israel encamped before them like two little flocks of goats, whereas, the Syrians, filled the land.

text.NRSV: A man of God approached and said to the king of Israel, “Thus says the Lord, Because the Arameans have said, ‘The Lord is a god of the hills but he is not a god of the valleys,’ therefore I will give all this great multitude into your hand, and you shall know that I am the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Then approached the man of God, and spake unto the king of Israel, and said—Thus, saith Yahweh—Because the Syrians have said—A god of the mountains, is Yahweh, but, not a god of the vales, is he, therefore will I deliver all this great multitude, into thy hand, so shalt thou know, that, I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: They encamped opposite each other seven days. Then on the seventh day the battle was joined; the Israelites killed one hundred thousand Aramean foot soldiers in one day.
text.Rotherham: So they encamped, these, over against, those, seven days,—and it came to pass, on the seventh day, that the battle was joined, and the sons of Israel smote the Syrians, a hundred thousand footmen, in one day.

text.NRSV: The rest fled into the city of Aphek; and the wall fell on twenty-seven thousand men who were left. Ben-hadad also fled, and entered the city to hide in an inner chamber.
text.Rotherham: And they who were left fled to Aphek, into the city, and the wall fell upon twenty-seven thousand men who were left,—and, Ben-hadad, fled, and came into the city, into a chamber within a chamber.

text.NRSV: His servants said to him, “Look, we have heard that the kings of the house of Israel are merciful kings; let us put sackcloth around our waists and ropes on our heads, and go out to the king of Israel; perhaps he will spare your life.”
text.Rotherham: And his servants said unto him, Lo! we pray thee, we have heard, of the kings of the house of Israel, that, kings known for lovingkindness, they are. Let us, we pray thee, put sackcloth upon our loins, and ropes about our head, and let us go forth unto the king of Israel, peradventure he will save alive thy soul.

text.NRSV: So they tied sackcloth around their waists and put ropes on their heads, went to the king of Israel, and said, “Your servant Ben-hadad says, ‘Please let me live.’” And he said, “Is he still alive? He is my brother.”
text.Rotherham: So they girded sackcloth upon their loins, and [put] ropes about their heads, and came in unto the king of Israel, and said, Thy servant, Ben-hadad, saith,—Let my soul live, I pray thee. And he said,—Is he yet alive? My brother, he is.

text.NRSV: Now the men were watching for an omen; they quickly took it up from him and said, “Yes, Ben-hadad is your brother.” Then he said, “Go and bring him.” So Ben-hadad came out to him; and he had him come up into the chariot.
text.Rotherham: Now, the men, could divine, so they hastened to let him confirm the word of his own accord, and they said,—Thy brother, is Ben-hadad! He said therefore,—Go fetch him. So Ben-hadad came forth unto him, and he made him come up unto him on his chariot.

text.NRSV: Ben-hadad said to him, “The cities that my father took from your father I will restore; and you may establish bazaars for yourself in Damascus, as my father did in Samaria.” To which Ahab replied, “I will let you go on these terms.” So he made a treaty with him and let him go.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him—The cities which my father took from thy father, will I restore, and, bazaars, shalt thou make thee in Damascus, as my father made in Samaria. So then, I, with this covenant, will let thee go. So he solemnised with him a covenant, and let him go.

text.NRSV: At the command of the Lord a certain member of a company of prophets said to another, “Strike me!” But the man refused to strike him.
text.Rotherham: And, a certain man of the sons of the prophets, said unto his neighbour, by the word of Yahweh—Smite me, I pray thee. But the man refused to smite him.

text.NRSV: Then he said to him, “Because you have not obeyed the voice of the Lord, as soon as you have left me, a lion shall kill you.” And when he had left him, a lion met him and killed him.
text.Rotherham: So he said to him—Because thou hast not hearkened unto the voice of Yahweh, lo! when thou art departing from me, there shall smite thee a lion. And when he departed from beside him, a lion found him, and smote him.

text.NRSV: Then he found another man and said, “Strike me!” So the man struck him, striking and wounding him.
text.Rotherham: Then found he another man, and said,—Smite me, I pray thee. So the man smote him—kept on smiting and wounding.

text.NRSV: So the prophet departed, and waited for the king on the road, disguising himself with a bandage over his eyes.
text.Rotherham: Then the prophet departed, and waited for the king, by the way,—and disguised himself with his turban over his eyes.

text.NRSV: As the king passed by, he cried to the king and said, “Your servant went out into the thick of the battle; then a soldier turned and brought a man to me and said, ‘Guard this man; if for any reason he is missing, your life shall be for his life, or else you shall pay a talent of silver.’
text.Rotherham: And so it was, when, the king, was passing, he, cried out unto the king,—and said—Thy servant, went out in the midst of the battle, and lo! a man, turned aside and brought unto me a man, and said—Keep this man, if he be, missing, then shall, thy life, go for, his life, or, a talent of silver, shalt thou weigh out.

text.NRSV: While your servant was busy here and there, he was gone.” The king of Israel said to him, “So shall your judgment be; you yourself have decided it.”
text.Rotherham: And so it was, as thy servant was busy here and there, that, he, was gone. And the king of Israel said unto him—Such, is thy judgment, thou thyself, hast decided it.

text.NRSV: Then he quickly took the bandage away from his eyes. The king of Israel recognized him as one of the prophets.
text.Rotherham: Then hastened he, and removed the turban from over his eyes,—and the king of Israel knew him, that, of the prophets, was, he.

text.NRSV: Then he said to him, “Thus says the Lord, Because you have let the man go whom I had devoted to destruction, therefore your life shall be for his life, and your people for his people.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him—Thus, saith Yahweh, Because thou hast let go the man whom I had devoted, out of thy hand, therefore shall, thy life, be instead of, his life, and, thy people, instead of, his people.

text.NRSV: The king of Israel set out for his house, resentful and sullen, and came to Samaria.
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel departed unto his house, sullen and disturbed,—and entered Samaria.

text.NRSV: Later the following events took place: Naboth the Jezreelite had a vineyard in Jezreel, beside the palace of King Ahab of Samaria.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after these things, that Naboth the Jezreelite had, a vineyard, which was in Jezreel,—hard by the palace of Ahab, king of Samaria.

text.NRSV: And Ahab said to Naboth, “Give me your vineyard, so that I may have it for a vegetable garden, because it is near my house; I will give you a better vineyard for it; or, if it seems good to you, I will give you its value in money.”
text.Rotherham: Ahab, therefore, spake unto Naboth, saying—Come! give me thy vineyard, that I may have it for a garden of herbs, for, the same, is near by my house, and let me give thee, instead thereof, a vineyard better than it,—if it be good in thine eyes, I will give thee silver to the value of this.

text.NRSV: But Naboth said to Ahab, “The Lord forbid that I should give you my ancestral inheritance.”
text.Rotherham: And Naboth said unto Ahab,—Far be it from me, of Yahweh! that I should give up the inheritance of my fathers, unto thee!

text.NRSV: Ahab went home resentful and sullen because of what Naboth the Jezreelite had said to him; for he had said, “I will not give you my ancestral inheritance.” He lay down on his bed, turned away his face, and would not eat.
text.Rotherham: So Ahab came into his house, sullen and disturbed, because of the word which Naboth the Jezreelite had spoken unto him, that he should have said, I will not give thee the inheritance of my fathers. So he laid him down upon his bed, and turned away his face, and did eat no food.

text.NRSV: His wife Jezebel came to him and said, “Why are you so depressed that you will not eat?”
text.Rotherham: Then came unto him Jezebel his wife,—and said unto him—Why is it, that thy spirit is sullen, that thou art, not eating food?

text.NRSV: He said to her, “Because I spoke to Naboth the Jezreelite and said to him, ‘Give me your vineyard for money; or else, if you prefer, I will give you another vineyard for it’; but he answered, ‘I will not give you my vineyard.’”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto her—Because I spake unto Naboth, the Jezreelite, and said unto him—Come! give me thy vineyard for silver, or, if thou wouldst prefer, I will give thee a vineyard, in its stead; and he said—I will not give thee my vineyard.

text.NRSV: His wife Jezebel said to him, “Do you now govern Israel? Get up, eat some food, and be cheerful; I will give you the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite.”
text.Rotherham: Then Jezebel his wife said unto him: Art, thou, still, going to carry on the kingdom over Israel? Rise! eat food, and let thy heart be merry, I, will give thee the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite!

text.NRSV: So she wrote letters in Ahab’s name and sealed them with his seal; she sent the letters to the elders and the nobles who lived with Naboth in his city.
text.Rotherham: So she wrote letters, in the name of Ahab, and sealed them with his signet-ring,—and sent the letters unto the elders, and unto the nobles who were in his city, dwelling with Naboth.

text.NRSV: She wrote in the letters, “Proclaim a fast, and seat Naboth at the head of the assembly;
text.Rotherham: Now she wrote in the letters, saying,—Proclaim ye a fast, and cause Naboth to sit at the head of the people;

text.NRSV: seat two scoundrels opposite him, and let them bring a charge against him, saying, ‘You have cursed God and the king.’ Then take him out, and stone him to death.”
text.Rotherham: then let two reckless men take their seats before him, that they may bear witness against him, saying, Thou hast reviled God and king! then shall ye carry him forth and stone him, that he die.

text.NRSV: The men of his city, the elders and the nobles who lived in his city, did as Jezebel had sent word to them. As it was written in the letters that she had sent to them,
text.Rotherham: So the men of his city, the elders and the nobles who dwelt in his city, did just as Jezebel had sent unto them,—as it was written in the letters which she had sent unto them:

text.NRSV: they proclaimed a fast and seated Naboth at the head of the assembly.
text.Rotherham: they proclaimed a fast,—and caused Naboth to sit at the head of the people.

text.NRSV: The two scoundrels came in and sat opposite him; and the scoundrels brought a charge against Naboth, in the presence of the people, saying, “Naboth cursed God and the king.” So they took him outside the city, and stoned him to death with stones.
text.Rotherham: Then came in the two reckless men, and sat before him, and the men bare witness against him, even against Naboth, before the people, saying, Naboth hath reviled God and king! So they carried him forth, outside the city, and stoned him with stones, that he died.

text.NRSV: Then they sent to Jezebel, saying, “Naboth has been stoned; he is dead.”
text.Rotherham: Then sent they unto Jezebel, saying,—Naboth is stoned and is dead.

text.NRSV: As soon as Jezebel heard that Naboth had been stoned and was dead, Jezebel said to Ahab, “Go, take possession of the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite, which he refused to give you for money; for Naboth is not alive, but dead.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Jezebel heard that Naboth was stoned and was dead, that Jezebel said unto Ahab—Rise! take possession of the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite, which he refused to give thee for silver, for Naboth is not alive, but dead!

text.NRSV: As soon as Ahab heard that Naboth was dead, Ahab set out to go down to the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite, to take possession of it.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Ahab heard that Naboth was dead, that Ahab arose, to go down unto the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite, to take possession thereof.

text.NRSV: Then the word of the Lord came to Elijah the Tishbite, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto Elijah the Tishbite, saying:

text.NRSV: “Go down to meet King Ahab of Israel, who rules in Samaria; he is now in the vineyard of Naboth, where he has gone to take possession.
text.Rotherham: Arise! go down to meet Ahab king of Israel, who is in Samaria,—lo! he is in the vineyard of Naboth, whither he hath gone down, to take possession thereof.

text.NRSV: You shall say to him, ‘Thus says the Lord: Have you killed, and also taken possession?’ You shall say to him, ‘Thus says the Lord: In the place where dogs licked up the blood of Naboth, dogs will also lick up your blood.’”
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou speak unto him, saying—Thus, saith Yahweh, Hast thou committed murder, and also taken possession? Then shalt thou speak unto him, saying: Thus, saith Yahweh, In the place where the dogs have lapped up the blood of Naboth, shall the dogs lap up thy blood, even thine.

text.NRSV: Ahab said to Elijah, “Have you found me, O my enemy?” He answered, “I have found you. Because you have sold yourself to do what is evil in the sight of the Lord,
text.Rotherham: Then said Ahab unto Elijah, Hast thou found me, O mine enemy? And he said: I have found [thee] ! Because thou hast sold thyself to do the thing that is wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,

text.NRSV: I will bring disaster on you; I will consume you, and will cut off from Ahab every male, bond or free, in Israel;
text.Rotherham: Behold me! [saith he] bringing in upon thee, calamity, and I will consume after thee,—and cut off of Ahab, even the meanest, whether shut up or left at large in Israel;

text.NRSV: and I will make your house like the house of Jeroboam son of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha son of Ahijah, because you have provoked me to anger and have caused Israel to sin.
text.Rotherham: and will deliver up thy house—Like the house of Jeroboam son of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha son of Ahijah,—for the provocation wherewith thou hast provoked, and caused, Israel, to sin.

text.NRSV: Also concerning Jezebel the Lord said, ‘The dogs shall eat Jezebel within the bounds of Jezreel.’
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, concerning Jezebel, hath Yahweh spoken, saying,—The dogs, shall eat Jezebel, in the town-land of Jezreel:

text.NRSV: Anyone belonging to Ahab who dies in the city the dogs shall eat; and anyone of his who dies in the open country the birds of the air shall eat.”
text.Rotherham: Him that dieth, of Ahab, in the city, shall, the dogs, eat,—and, him that dieth in the field, shall, the birds of heaven, eat.

text.NRSV: (Indeed, there was no one like Ahab, who sold himself to do what was evil in the sight of the Lord, urged on by his wife Jezebel.
text.Rotherham: But indeed, there was none like Ahab, who sold himself to do the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—whom Jezebel his wife goaded on;

text.NRSV: He acted most abominably in going after idols, as the Amorites had done, whom the Lord drove out before the Israelites.)
text.Rotherham: so that he did very abominably, in going after the manufactured gods,—according to all which the Amorites had done, whom Yahweh dispossessed from before the sons of Israel.

text.NRSV: When Ahab heard those words, he tore his clothes and put sackcloth over his bare flesh; he fasted, lay in the sackcloth, and went about dejectedly.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Ahab heard these words, that he rent his clothes, and put sackcloth upon his flesh, and fasted,—and lay in sackcloth, and went softly.

text.NRSV: Then the word of the Lord came to Elijah the Tishbite:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto Elijah the Tishbite, saying:

text.NRSV: “Have you seen how Ahab has humbled himself before me? Because he has humbled himself before me, I will not bring the disaster in his days; but in his son’s days I will bring the disaster on his house.”
text.Rotherham: Hast thou seen that Ahab hath humbled himself, before me? Because he hath humbled himself before me, I will not bring in the calamity in his days, In the days of his son, will I bring in the calamity upon his house.

text.NRSV: For three years Aram and Israel continued without war.
text.Rotherham: And there continued three years without war between Syria and Israel.

text.NRSV: But in the third year King Jehoshaphat of Judah came down to the king of Israel.
text.Rotherham: But it came to pass, in the third year, that Jehoshaphat king of Judah went down unto the king of Israel.

text.NRSV: The king of Israel said to his servants, “Do you know that Ramoth-gilead belongs to us, yet we are doing nothing to take it out of the hand of the king of Aram?”
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel said unto his servants, Know ye that, ours, is Ramoth-gilead,—yet, we, are too idle to take it out of the hand of the king of Syria?

text.NRSV: He said to Jehoshaphat, “Will you go with me to battle at Ramoth-gilead?” Jehoshaphat replied to the king of Israel, “I am as you are; my people are as your people, my horses are as your horses.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto Jehoshaphat, Wilt thou go with me to make war upon Ramoth-gilead?

text.NRSV: But Jehoshaphat also said to the king of Israel, “Inquire first for the word of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And Jehoshaphat said unto the king of Israel, I am as thou art, my people are us thy people, my horses as thy horses.

text.NRSV: Then the king of Israel gathered the prophets together, about four hundred of them, and said to them, “Shall I go to battle against Ramoth-gilead, or shall I refrain?” They said, “Go up; for the Lord will give it into the hand of the king.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Jehoshaphat unto the king of Israel,—Seek, I pray thee, at once, the word of Yahweh. So the king of Israel gathered together the prophets, about four hundred men, and said unto them—Shall I go against Ramoth-gilead, to battle, or shall I forbear? And they said—Go up, that the Lord may deliver it into the hand of the king.

text.NRSV: But Jehoshaphat said, “Is there no other prophet of the Lord here of whom we may inquire?”
text.Rotherham: Then said Jehoshaphat, Is there not here a prophet of Yahweh, besides,—that we may seek, from him?

text.NRSV: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “There is still one other by whom we may inquire of the Lord, Micaiah son of Imlah; but I hate him, for he never prophesies anything favorable about me, but only disaster.” Jehoshaphat said, “Let the king not say such a thing.”
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat, Besides, is a certain man, by whom we might seek Yahweh, but, I, hate him, for he is never moved to prophesy concerning me anything good, only evil, Micaiah, son of Imlah. And Jehoshaphat said, Let not the king say so.

text.NRSV: Then the king of Israel summoned an officer and said, “Bring quickly Micaiah son of Imlah.”
text.Rotherham: Then the king of Israel called a certain courtier,—and said, Hasten Micaiah son of Imlah.

text.NRSV: Now the king of Israel and King Jehoshaphat of Judah were sitting on their thrones, arrayed in their robes, at the threshing floor at the entrance of the gate of Samaria; and all the prophets were prophesying before them.
text.Rotherham: Now, the king of Israel, and Jehoshaphat king of Judah, were sitting—each man upon his throne, having put on robes, in a level place, at the entrance of the gate of Samaria,—and, all the prophets, were being moved to prophesy before them;

text.NRSV: Zedekiah son of Chenaanah made for himself horns of iron, and he said, “Thus says the Lord: With these you shall gore the Arameans until they are destroyed.”
text.Rotherham: when Zedekiah son of Chenaanah made himself horns of iron,—and said—Thus, saith Yahweh,—With these, shalt thou push down the Syrians, until thou hast consumed them.

text.NRSV: All the prophets were prophesying the same and saying, “Go up to Ramoth-gilead and triumph; the Lord will give it into the hand of the king.”
text.Rotherham: And, all the prophets, were being moved to prophesy, in like manner, saying,—Go up to Ramoth-gilead, and thou shalt prosper, and Yahweh will deliver it, into the hand of the king.

text.NRSV: The messenger who had gone to summon Micaiah said to him, “Look, the words of the prophets with one accord are favorable to the king; let your word be like the word of one of them, and speak favorably.”
text.Rotherham: Now, the messenger who went to call Micaiah, spake unto him, saying, Behold, I pray thee, the words of the prophets, with one mouth, are good, as touching the king, let thy word, I pray thee, be as the word of one of them, so wilt thou speak that which is good.

text.NRSV: But Micaiah said, “As the Lord lives, whatever the Lord says to me, that I will speak.”
text.Rotherham: And Micaiah said,—By the life of Yahweh, what Yahweh saith unto me, that, will I, speak.

text.NRSV: When he had come to the king, the king said to him, “Micaiah, shall we go to Ramoth-gilead to battle, or shall we refrain?” He answered him, “Go up and triumph; the Lord will give it into the hand of the king.”
text.Rotherham: So he came unto the king, and the king said unto him, Micaiah, shall we go against Ramoth-gilead to battle, or shall we forbear? And he said unto him—Go up and prosper, and Yahweh will deliver it into the hand of the king.

text.NRSV: But the king said to him, “How many times must I adjure you to tell me nothing but the truth in the name of the Lord?”
text.Rotherham: And the king said unto him, How many times must, I, adjure thee,—that thou speak unto me nothing but truth, in the name of Yahweh?

text.NRSV: Then Micaiah said, “I saw all Israel scattered on the mountains, like sheep that have no shepherd; and the Lord said, ‘These have no master; let each one go home in peace.’”
text.Rotherham: And he said—I saw all Israel, scattered among the mountains, like sheep that have no shepherd,—so Yahweh said, These have, no masters, let them return every man unto his own house, in peace.

text.NRSV: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “Did I not tell you that he would not prophesy anything favorable about me, but only disaster?”
text.Rotherham: Then said the king of Israel, unto Jehoshaphat,—Did I not say unto thee, He will not be moved to prophesy concerning me anything good, only calamity.

text.NRSV: Then Micaiah said, “Therefore hear the word of the Lord: I saw the Lord sitting on his throne, with all the host of heaven standing beside him to the right and to the left of him.
text.Rotherham: Then he said, Therefore, hear thou the word of Yahweh,—I saw Yahweh, sitting upon his throne, and all the host of the heavens, standing by him, on his right hand, and on his left.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said, ‘Who will entice Ahab, so that he may go up and fall at Ramoth-gilead?’ Then one said one thing, and another said another,
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said, Who will persuade Ahab, that he may go up and fall, at Ramoth-gilead? And, one, said in this manner, and, another, said in that manner.

text.NRSV: until a spirit came forward and stood before the Lord, saying, ‘I will entice him.’
text.Rotherham: Then came forth a spirit, and stood before Yahweh, and said—I, will persuade him.

text.NRSV: ‘How?’ the Lord asked him. He replied, ‘I will go out and be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets.’ Then the Lord said, ‘You are to entice him, and you shall succeed; go out and do it.’
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto him—Wherewith? And he said—I will go forth, and become a spirit of falsehood in the mouth of all his prophets,—So he said,—Thou mayest persuade, yea and prevail, go forth, and do so.

text.NRSV: So you see, the Lord has put a lying spirit in the mouth of all these your prophets; the Lord has decreed disaster for you.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, lo! Yahweh hath suffered a spirit of falsehood to be put into the mouth of all these thy prophets. But, Yahweh himself, hath spoken concerning thee, calamity.

text.NRSV: Then Zedekiah son of Chenaanah came up to Micaiah, struck him on the cheek, and said, “Which way did the spirit of the Lord go from me to speak to you?”
text.Rotherham: Then drew near Zedekiah, son of Chenaanah, and smote Micaiah on the cheek,—and said—Where then passed the Spirit of Yahweh, from me, to speak unto thee?

text.NRSV: Micaiah replied, “You will find out on that day when you go in to hide in an inner chamber.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Micaiah, Lo! thou art about to see, on that day,—when thou enterest a chamber within a chamber, to hide thyself.

text.NRSV: The king of Israel then ordered, “Take Micaiah, and return him to Amon the governor of the city and to Joash the king’s son,
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel said, Take Micaiah, and carry him back unto Amon captain of the city,—and unto Joash son of the king;

text.NRSV: and say, ‘Thus says the king: Put this fellow in prison, and feed him on reduced rations of bread and water until I come in peace.’”
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt say—Thus, saith the king, Put this man into the prison,—and let him eat the bread of oppression, with the water of oppression, until I enter in peace.

text.NRSV: Micaiah said, “If you return in peace, the Lord has not spoken by me.” And he said, “Hear, you peoples, all of you!”
text.Rotherham: Then said Micaiah, If thou, return, in peace, Yahweh hath not spoken by me. And he said, Hear, ye peoples, all of you!

text.NRSV: So the king of Israel and King Jehoshaphat of Judah went up to Ramoth-gilead.
text.Rotherham: So the king of Israel went up, with Jehoshaphat king of Judah, unto Ramoth-gilead.

text.NRSV: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “I will disguise myself and go into battle, but you wear your robes.” So the king of Israel disguised himself and went into the battle.
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat—I am about to disguise myself, and enter into the battle, thou, therefore, put on thy robes. So the king of Israel disguised himself, and entered into the battle.

text.NRSV: Now the king of Aram had commanded the thirty-two captains of his chariots, “Fight with no one small or great, but only with the king of Israel.”
text.Rotherham: Now, the king of Syria, had commanded the captains of chariots which he had, thirty and two, saying, Ye shall not fight with small or great,—save with the king of Israel alone.

text.NRSV: When the captains of the chariots saw Jehoshaphat, they said, “It is surely the king of Israel.” So they turned to fight against him; and Jehoshaphat cried out.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the chariot-captains saw Jehoshaphat, that, they, said: Surely it is, the king of Israel! But, when they turned aside against him to fight, Jehoshaphat cried out.

text.NRSV: When the captains of the chariots saw that it was not the king of Israel, they turned back from pursuing him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the chariot-captains saw that it was, not the king of Israel, that they turned back, from pursuing him.

text.NRSV: But a certain man drew his bow and unknowingly struck the king of Israel between the scale armor and the breastplate; so he said to the driver of his chariot, “Turn around, and carry me out of the battle, for I am wounded.”
text.Rotherham: But, a certain man, drawing a bow in his innocence, smote the king of Israel, between the shoulder-joints and the coat of mail,—wherefore he said to his charioteer—Turn thy hand, and convey me out of the host, for I am sore wounded.

text.NRSV: The battle raged that day, and the king was propped up in his chariot facing the Arameans, until at evening he died; the blood from the wound had flowed into the bottom of the chariot.
text.Rotherham: But the battle increased that day, and, the king, was propped up in the chariot, before the Syrians,—and died in the evening, and so the blood of the wound ran out into the hollow of the chariot.

text.NRSV: Then about sunset a shout went through the army, “Every man to his city, and every man to his country!”
text.Rotherham: And a loud cry went through the host, at the going in of the sun, saying—Every man to his own city! and every man to his own land!

text.NRSV: So the king died, and was brought to Samaria; they buried the king in Samaria.
text.Rotherham: So the king died, and was brought into Samaria,—and they buried the king, in Samaria.

text.NRSV: They washed the chariot by the pool of Samaria; the dogs licked up his blood, and the prostitutes washed themselves in it, according to the word of the Lord that he had spoken.
text.Rotherham: And, when the chariot was washed out at the pool of Samaria, the dogs lapped up his blood, also, the harlots, bathed [there],—according to the word of Yahweh which he had spoken.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Ahab, and all that he did, and the ivory house that he built, and all the cities that he built, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Ahab, and all that he did, and the house of ivory that he built, and all the cities that he built, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the kings of Israel?

text.NRSV: So Ahab slept with his ancestors; and his son Ahaziah succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: So Ahab slept with his fathers,—and, Ahaziah his son, reigned, in his stead.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat son of Asa began to reign over Judah in the fourth year of King Ahab of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, Jehoshaphat son of Asa, began to reign over Judah,—in the fourth year of Ahab, king of Israel.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat was thirty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty-five years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Azubah daughter of Shilhi.
text.Rotherham: Jehoshaphat, was thirty-five years old when he began to reign, and, twenty-five years, reigned he in Jerusalem,—and, the name of his mother, was Azubah, daughter of Shilhi.

text.NRSV: He walked in all the way of his father Asa; he did not turn aside from it, doing what was right in the sight of the Lord; yet the high places were not taken away, and the people still sacrificed and offered incense on the high places.
text.Rotherham: And he walked in all the way of Asa his father, he turned not aside therefrom,—doing that which was right in the eyes of Yahweh: nevertheless, the high places, were not taken away,—still were the people offering sacrifices and burning incense in the high places.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat also made peace with the king of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoshaphat made peace with the king of Israel.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat, and his power that he showed, and how he warred, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Judah?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Jehoshaphat, and his might that he shewed, and how he warred, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the kings of Judah?

text.NRSV: The remnant of the male temple prostitutes who were still left in the days of his father Asa, he exterminated from the land.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, the rest of the male devotees who remained in the days of Asa his father, he consumed out of the land.

text.NRSV: There was no king in Edom; a deputy was king.
text.Rotherham: And, king, was there none in Edom, a prefect, was king.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat made ships of Tarshish to go to Ophir for gold; but they did not go, for the ships were wrecked at Ezion-geber.
text.Rotherham: Jehoshaphat, made ships of Tarshish, to go to Ophir, for gold; but they went not,—for ships had been broken to pieces in Ezion-geber.

text.NRSV: Then Ahaziah son of Ahab said to Jehoshaphat, “Let my servants go with your servants in the ships,” but Jehoshaphat was not willing.
text.Rotherham: Then, said Ahaziah son of Ahab, unto Jehoshaphat, Let, my servants, go with, thy servants, in the ships,—but Jehoshaphat did not consent.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat slept with his ancestors and was buried with his ancestors in the city of his father David; his son Jehoram succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: So Jehoshaphat slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers, in the city of David his father,—and, Jehoram his son, reigned, in his stead.

text.NRSV: Ahaziah son of Ahab began to reign over Israel in Samaria in the seventeenth year of King Jehoshaphat of Judah; he reigned two years over Israel.
text.Rotherham: Ahaziah son of Ahab, began to reign over Israel, in Samaria, in the seventeenth year of Jehoshaphat king of Judah,—and reigned over Israel, two years.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, and walked in the way of his father and mother, and in the way of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who caused Israel to sin.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—and went in the way of his father, and in the way of his mother, and in the way of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who caused, Israel, to sin.

text.NRSV: He served Baal and worshiped him, and provoked the Lord, the God of Israel, to anger, just as his father had done.
text.Rotherham: Yea he served Baal, and bowed down to him,—and provoked to anger Yahweh, God of Israel, according to all that his father had done.
